summary: Iteration 3 — focused re-verification of iteration_2 issue #1 fix (order-number stepper auto-refresh on /home after print). PASS: home.tsx now uses useFocusEffect from expo-router that re-reads getOrderNumber() and getPrinter() on every focus. Flow verified end-to-end on the Expo web preview at 375x667: cleared storage → first-launch routed to /printer-setup → tapped printer-connect-DEMO-58-01 → landed on /home with order-number-value='#1' → typed 'Arvind' into home-customer-input → tapped generate-button → /preview rendered → tapped print-button → preview-toast 'Printed (simulated). Build APK for real BT printing.' appeared → preview-back → /home order-number-value='#2' ✅. Repeated: generate → print → back → order-number-value='#3' ✅. No regression: order-increment/order-decrement still mutate local state (#3→#4→#3), open-settings navigates correctly, settings-printer-name visible, settings-back returns to /home and the focus effect re-reads storage so the stepper still shows the persisted '#3'. Known web-preview-only artifact (acknowledged in request): CORS still blocks the quote fetch so preview-quote shows 'Failed to fetch' — does not affect the order-number flow because preview.tsx onPrint uses the order_number route param (not the quote) when bumping storage. Deprecated packages: none. SafeAreaView issues detected: none.
action_items: None for the targeted fix — iteration_2 issue #1 is fully resolved.

OPTIONAL polish carried over from iteration_2 (non-blocking): replace deprecated TouchableWithoutFeedback in home.tsx with Pressable, replace shadow* style props with boxShadow on preview.tsx noteShadow, replace props.pointerEvents with style.pointerEvents on preview-toast. Console warnings only, no functional impact.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/home.tsx:38-51 — correctly uses useFocusEffect(useCallback(..., [])) with an `alive` cancel flag and Promise.all([getOrderNumber(), getPrinter()]). Clean, idiomatic Expo Router pattern. No stale-closure or memory-leak concerns.
/app/frontend/app/preview.tsx:94-95 — onPrint persists `Math.max(cur, orderNumber) + 1` before navigating back, so the next focus on /home correctly sees the incremented value.
/app/frontend/src/lib/storage.ts — unchanged from iteration_2; AsyncStorage write inside saveOrderNumber is awaited, so the read on /home focus is guaranteed to see the new value.

success_rate.frontend: Targeted re-test: 3/3 pass (#1→#2 after 1st print, #2→#3 after 2nd print, no regressions on stepper / settings / printer-chip / generate-button / print-button / preview-toast / preview-back)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix for iteration_2 ui_bug #1 is verified and shipped. The only outstanding item (web-preview CORS on https://expertdevelopers.in/generate-8858-quote-for-sfj → preview-quote='Failed to fetch') is a browser-only artifact intentionally accepted by the request — real Expo Go / built APK will not hit CORS. All previously verified testIDs still resolve. Order persistence formula is: home reads from storage on focus; preview writes `max(stored, route_order)+1` on print. Stepper local increments are NOT persisted until generate-button runs (saveOrderNumber(order)), which is the intended behavior.
rca_of_issue: N/A — iteration_2 RCA #1 has been mitigated. Verified by direct DOM observation of [data-testid='order-number-value'] before/after the focus event triggered by router.back().
